title: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 2
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 14-26, which are based on Reading Passage 2 below.

Natural Ways to Clean Water

A

In 2026, engineers and health workers are paying renewed attention to water-cleaning methods that imitate processes found in nature. The interest is not merely academic. International agencies estimate that more than two billion people still use drinking-water sources that may be contaminated, and many of them live in places where electricity, chemical supplies or replacement parts are unreliable. A treatment system that depends on a pump, a constant power supply or imported tablets can fail just when floods, droughts or conflict make safe water most urgent. For this reason, a growing number of projects are testing low-energy filters that use seeds, sand, charcoal and gravity rather than complex machinery.

B

One promising approach began with a familiar observation in parts of East Africa and South Asia: crushed seeds from the moringa tree can make muddy water clearer. For generations, some households had stirred the seed powder into pots of river water before leaving the mixture to settle. Researchers noticed that proteins in the seeds carry a slight electrical charge, allowing them to attract tiny particles that normally remain suspended in water. From this traditional practice, a team of designers developed a small household filter in which moringa powder is combined with layers of sand and charcoal. Their aim was to turn an occasional home remedy into a repeatable product that could be used safely every day.

C

The method differs from many alternatives because it does not try to kill every organism immediately with a strong chemical. Instead, it first helps dirt, organic matter and some microbes gather into larger clumps, which can then sink as sediment or be trapped by the filter bed. Chlorine tablets can be effective, but they may leave a taste that users dislike and they have to be bought regularly. Boiling water can work too, but it consumes fuel and may increase smoke inside homes. The seed-sand filter is designed to reduce cloudiness and remove much of the material that shelters bacteria before any final disinfection is considered. In other words, it works with a natural binding process rather than replacing it with a purely chemical one.

D

Field teams have tested the system on water from several sources: village wells, roof tanks, canals, ponds and floodwater collected after storms. It is not presented as a magic solution for every possible contaminant. Industrial chemicals, salt and some dissolved metals may still require specialist treatment. Even so, tests suggest that the filter can greatly improve water that is cloudy because of soil, plant material or animal waste. This matters because many communities do not face one neat water problem. A household may draw from a well in the dry season, a pond during a drought and a temporary tank during a disaster response. A flexible filter is therefore more useful than one designed for a single source.

E

The possible users are varied. Rural families may use the filter at home, while schools can place a larger version near hand-washing stations or kitchens. Health clinics are interested because they need safer water for patients and cleaning, especially when public supplies are interrupted. Emergency organisations have also considered the design for temporary camps, where large treatment plants cannot be built quickly. Small farmers, meanwhile, may use the first, less pure output for washing tools or watering seedlings, reserving the cleanest water for drinking. The designers argue that this range of customers is important because a product made only for aid agencies can disappear when a grant ends.

F

Turning a natural idea into a reliable system has not been simple. Moringa seeds vary in strength according to variety, storage and age, so one spoonful of powder may not always treat the same amount of water. Early versions also became messy when users added too much powder, leaving an organic smell in stored water. To overcome these production difficulties, the team now dries the seeds under controlled conditions, grinds them into a fine powder and packs the powder in measured sachets. Each sachet gives a clear dose for a standard bucket. The filter body has also been redesigned so that the sand layer can be cleaned without dismantling the whole unit, and the treated water is kept in a covered container after filtration.

G

The project has attracted commercial interest, but its founders say profit is not the primary goal. They want local workshops to make the filter body, local growers to supply seeds and local health workers to teach safe use. A high price would defeat the purpose, because the households most at risk from dirty water are often least able to pay for imported equipment. The founders therefore use a mixed model: wealthier institutions pay the full cost, while community programmes receive filters at a reduced price. They hope the business will survive, but they measure success mainly by whether families continue to drink safer water after the first demonstration ends.

H

A small trial produced an unexpected benefit. Because users could see muddy water becoming clearer within minutes, the filter became a teaching tool. Children asked why the dirt was sinking, and health workers used the visible change to explain invisible risks such as bacteria. Several villages welcomed the device not only because the water looked better, but because the process was easy to understand. The researchers caution that clear water is not always safe water, and they continue to recommend testing and, where necessary, final disinfection. Still, they believe a natural method that people can observe may encourage more consistent treatment than a method that seems mysterious.

Questions 14-20

Reading Passage 2 has eight paragraphs, A-H.

Choose the correct heading for each section from the list of headings below.

Write the correct number, i-x, in boxes 14-20 on your answer sheet.

List of Headings

i Getting the finance for production

ii An unexpected benefit

iii From initial inspiration to new product

iv The range of potential customers for the device

v What makes the device different from alternatives

vi Cleaning water from a range of sources

vii Overcoming production difficulties

viii Profit not the primary goal

ix A warm welcome for the device

x The number of people affected by unsafe water

14 Section C
15 Section B
16 Section A
17 Section D
18 Section E
19 Section F
20 Section G

Questions 21-26

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Write your answers in boxes 21-26 on your answer sheet.

How the seed-sand filter works

The system uses a natural process found in moringa seeds. Crushed seeds produce a 21 .................... that can be combined with layers of 22 .................... and charcoal. This natural process makes small particles join together into larger clumps, and some of these sink as 23 .................... . To make the treatment reliable, each sachet gives a clear 24 .................... for a standard bucket. To keep the treated water safe, it should be kept in a covered 25 .................... . In some cases, final 26 .................... may still be recommended before the water is drunk.
rawAnswerKey:
Question 14: Section C

Correct Answer: v

Accepted Answers: v

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: The method differs from many alternatives because it does not try to kill every organism immediately with a strong chemical

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ v เพราะย่อหน้า C อธิบายว่าวิธีนี้ต่างจากทางเลือกอื่นอย่างไร โดยเปรียบเทียบกับ chlorine tablets และ boiling หลักฐานคือ “The method differs from many alternatives...” จึงตรงกับ what makes the device different from alternatives หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “The method differs from many alternatives because it does not try to kill every organism immediately with a strong chemical” ซึ่งบอกใจความหลักของ section นี้โดยตรง ดังนั้นต้องเลือก heading v เพราะ heading นี้สรุปประเด็นรวมของย่อหน้าได้ครบที่สุด ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงคำเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: different from alternatives = differs from many alternatives; chemical treatment = strong chemical / chlorine tablets; answer signal = v

Question 15: Section B

Correct Answer: iii

Accepted Answers: iii

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: From this traditional practice, a team of designers developed a small household filter in which moringa powder is combined with layers of sand and charcoal

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ iii เพราะย่อหน้า B เริ่มจากแรงบันดาลใจ/ภูมิปัญญาเดิมเรื่องเมล็ด moringa แล้วพัฒนาเป็น household filter หลักฐานคือ “From this traditional practice, a team of designers developed a small household filter...” จึงตรงกับ From initial inspiration to new product

Paraphrased Vocabulary: initial inspiration = traditional practice / familiar observation; new product = small household filter; answer signal = iii

Question 16: Section A

Correct Answer: x

Accepted Answers: x

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: International agencies estimate that more than two billion people still use drinking-water sources that may be contaminated

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ x เพราะย่อหน้า A ให้ภาพรวมของจำนวนคนที่ได้รับผลกระทบจากน้ำไม่ปลอดภัย โดยหลักฐานคือ “International agencies estimate that more than two billion people still use drinking-water sources that may be contaminated” จึงตรงกับ heading x เรื่องจำนวนคนที่ได้รับผลกระทบจาก unsafe water

Paraphrased Vocabulary: the number of people affected = more than two billion people; unsafe water = drinking-water sources that may be contaminated; answer signal = x

Question 17: Section D

Correct Answer: vi

Accepted Answers: vi

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: Field teams have tested the system on water from several sources: village wells, roof tanks, canals, ponds and floodwater collected after storms

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ vi เพราะย่อหน้า D พูดถึงการทดสอบกับน้ำจากหลายแหล่ง เช่น wells, tanks, canals, ponds และ floodwater หลักฐานคือ “water from several sources” จึงตรงกับ cleaning water from a range of sources หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “Field teams have tested the system on water from several sources: village wells, roof tanks, canals, ponds and floodwater collected after storms” ซึ่งบอกใจความหลักของ section นี้โดยตรง ดังนั้นต้องเลือก heading vi เพราะ heading นี้สรุปประเด็นรวมของย่อหน้าได้ครบที่สุด ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงคำเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: range of sources = several sources; cleaning water = tested the system on water; answer signal = vi

Question 18: Section E

Correct Answer: iv

Accepted Answers: iv

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: The possible users are varied. Rural families may use the filter at home, while schools can place a larger version near hand-washing stations or kitchens

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ iv เพราะย่อหน้า E แจกแจงกลุ่มผู้ใช้หลายประเภท เช่น families, schools, clinics, emergency organisations และ farmers หลักฐานคือ “The possible users are varied” จึงตรงกับ range of potential customers หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “The possible users are varied. Rural families may use the filter at home, while schools can place a larger version near hand-washing stations or kitchens” ซึ่งบอกใจความหลักของ section นี้โดยตรง ดังนั้นต้องเลือก heading iv เพราะ heading นี้สรุปประเด็นรวมของย่อหน้าได้ครบที่สุด ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงคำเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: potential customers = possible users; range = varied; device = filter; answer signal = iv

Question 19: Section F

Correct Answer: vii

Accepted Answers: vii

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: To overcome these production difficulties, the team now dries the seeds under controlled conditions, grinds them into a fine powder and packs the powder in measured sachets

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ vii เพราะย่อหน้า F อธิบายปัญหาในการผลิตและวิธีแก้ เช่น ความแรงของ seed powder ไม่คงที่และการจัด sachets หลักฐานคือ “To overcome these production difficulties...” จึงตรงกับ overcoming production difficulties หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “To overcome these production difficulties, the team now dries the seeds under controlled conditions, grinds them into a fine powder and packs the powder in measured sachets” ซึ่งบอกใจความหลักของ section นี้โดยตรง ดังนั้นต้องเลือก heading vii เพราะ heading นี้สรุปประเด็นรวมของย่อหน้าได้ครบที่สุด ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงคำเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: overcoming = to overcome; production difficulties = production difficulties; answer signal = vii

Question 20: Section G

Correct Answer: viii

Accepted Answers: viii

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: The project has attracted commercial interest, but its founders say profit is not the primary goal

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ viii เพราะย่อหน้า G ระบุชัดว่าแม้มีความสนใจเชิงพาณิชย์ แต่ profit is not the primary goal จึงตรงกับ heading viii หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “The project has attracted commercial interest, but its founders say profit is not the primary goal” ซึ่งบอกใจความหลักของ section นี้โดยตรง ดังนั้นต้องเลือก heading viii เพราะ heading นี้สรุปประเด็นรวมของย่อหน้าได้ครบที่สุด ไม่ใช่เลือกจากคำที่ดูคล้ายเพียงคำเดียว

Paraphrased Vocabulary: profit not the primary goal = profit is not the primary goal; commercial interest = attracted commercial interest; answer signal = viii

Question 21: The system uses a natural process found in moringa seeds. Crushed seeds produce a .................... that can be combined with layers of sand and charcoal.

Correct Answer: powder

Accepted Answers: powder

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: crushed seeds from the moringa tree can make muddy water clearer. For generations, some households had stirred the seed powder into pots of river water

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ powder เพราะ passage ระบุว่า crushed seeds from the moringa tree ใช้เป็น seed powder เพื่อทำให้น้ำขุ่นใสขึ้น และต่อมาย่อหน้าเดียวกันอธิบายว่า moringa powder ถูกใช้ร่วมกับชั้น sand และ charcoal ดังนั้นคำที่เติมคือ powder

Paraphrased Vocabulary: crushed seeds produce = seed powder; can be combined = is combined; answer signal = powder

Question 22: The water then passes through layers of .................... and charcoal.

Correct Answer: sand

Accepted Answers: sand

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: moringa powder is combined with layers of sand and charcoal

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ sand เพราะ summary พูดถึง layers of ____ and charcoal และ passage ใช้วลี “layers of sand and charcoal” โดยตรง จึงเติม sand หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “moringa powder is combined with layers of sand and charcoal” คำที่ต้องเติมคือ sand เพราะเป็นคำจาก passage โดยตรง เข้ากับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคใน summary และตรงตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY จึงไม่ควรใช้คำพ้องหรือเพิ่มคำอื่น

Paraphrased Vocabulary: passes through layers = combined with layers; charcoal = charcoal; answer signal = sand

Question 23: This makes small particles join together into larger clumps, and some of these sink as .................... .

Correct Answer: sediment

Accepted Answers: sediment

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: it first helps dirt, organic matter and some microbes gather into larger clumps, which can then sink as sediment

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ sediment เพราะ summary บอกว่า larger clumps sink as ____ และ passage ระบุ “larger clumps, which can then sink as sediment” ตรงตัว คำนี้พอดีกับช่องว่างหลัง as และเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “it first helps dirt, organic matter and some microbes gather into larger clumps, which can then sink as sediment” คำที่ต้องเติมคือ sediment เพราะเป็นคำจาก passage โดยตรง เข้ากับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคใน summary และตรงตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY จึงไม่ควรใช้คำพ้องหรือเพิ่มคำอื่น

Paraphrased Vocabulary: larger clumps = gather into larger clumps; sink as = sink as; answer signal = sediment

Question 24: A measured .................... is mixed with untreated water in a bucket.

Correct Answer: dose

Accepted Answers: dose

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: Each sachet gives a clear dose for a standard bucket

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ dose เพราะ summary ถามสิ่งที่ measured และผสมกับน้ำใน bucket หลักฐานคือ “Each sachet gives a clear dose for a standard bucket” จึงต้องเติม dose หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “Each sachet gives a clear dose for a standard bucket” คำที่ต้องเติมคือ dose เพราะเป็นคำจาก passage โดยตรง เข้ากับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคใน summary และตรงตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY จึงไม่ควรใช้คำพ้องหรือเพิ่มคำอื่น

Paraphrased Vocabulary: measured = clear; bucket = standard bucket; answer signal = dose

Question 25: To keep the treated water safe, it should be kept in a covered .................... .

Correct Answer: container

Accepted Answers: container

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: the treated water is kept in a covered container after filtration

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ container เพราะ summary ถามว่า treated water should be kept in a covered ____ และ passage ระบุ “the treated water is kept in a covered container after filtration” ตรงตัว คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์และเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “the treated water is kept in a covered container after filtration” คำที่ต้องเติมคือ container เพราะเป็นคำจาก passage โดยตรง เข้ากับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคใน summary และตรงตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY จึงไม่ควรใช้คำพ้องหรือเพิ่มคำอื่น

Paraphrased Vocabulary: kept = kept; treated water = treated water; covered container = covered container; answer signal = container

Question 26: In some cases, final .................... may still be recommended before the water is drunk.

Correct Answer: disinfection

Accepted Answers: disinfection

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 2 - Natural Ways to Clean Water

Exact Portion: they continue to recommend testing and, where necessary, final disinfection

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ disinfection เพราะ summary บอกว่า final ____ may still be recommended และ passage ระบุ “where necessary, final disinfection” ตรงตัว จึงตอบ disinfection หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “they continue to recommend testing and, where necessary, final disinfection” คำที่ต้องเติมคือ disinfection เพราะเป็นคำจาก passage โดยตรง เข้ากับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคใน summary และตรงตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY จึงไม่ควรใช้คำพ้องหรือเพิ่มคำอื่น

Paraphrased Vocabulary: in some cases = where necessary; may still be recommended = continue to recommend; answer signal = disinfection
